title: RSA migration: capture PKCS#1v1.5 baseline vectors
description:
Before touching any RSA code, capture deterministic baseline test vectors for PKCS#1v1.5 sign/verify. PKCS#1v1.5 is fully deterministic so we can do exact byte cross-validation between old EVP path and new native path.

What to capture:
- Generate a fixed 2048-bit and 4096-bit RSA key pair (seed the RNG or use fixed keys from a test vector set)
- Sign a short message with SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512 using current EVP path
- Record: input message, key (PKCS#1 DER), hash algo, expected signature bytes (hex)
- Store as hardcoded test vectors in wolfcrypt-conformance

Acceptance criteria:
- Test vectors captured and committed to wolfcrypt-conformance before any RSA migration code is written
- At minimum 2 key sizes × 3 hash algorithms = 6 vectors
- Tests pass with current EVP-based implementation (green baseline)
